2603.29454.title: Authorship Impersonation via LLM Prompting does not Evade Authorship Verification Methods
2603.29454.paper_summary: Authorship verification (AV), the task of determining whether a questioned text was written by a specific individual, is a critical part of forensic linguistics. While manual authorial impersonation by perpetrators has long been a recognized threat in historical forensic cases, recent advances in large language models (LLMs) raise new challenges, as adversaries may exploit these tools to impersonate another's writing. This study investigates whether prompted LLMs can generate convincing authorial impersonations and whether such outputs can evade existing forensic AV systems. Using GPT-4o as the adversary model, we generated impersonation texts under four prompting conditions across three genres: emails, text messages, and social media posts. We then evaluated these outputs against both non-neural AV methods (n-gram tracing, Ranking-Based Impostors Method, LambdaG) and neural approaches (AdHominem, LUAR, STAR) within a likelihood-ratio framework. Results show that LLM-generated texts failed to sufficiently replicate authorial individuality to bypass established AV systems. We also observed that some methods achieved even higher accuracy when rejecting impersonation texts compared to genuine negative samples. Overall, these findings indicate that, despite the accessibility of LLMs, current AV systems remain robust against entry-level impersonation attempts across multiple genres. Furthermore, we demonstrate that this counter-intuitive resilience stems, at least in part, from the higher lexical diversity and entropy inherent in LLM-generated texts.
2603.29454.paper_summary_zh: 作者驗證（AV），即確定一段有爭議的文本是否由特定個人撰寫的任務，是法醫語言學中的一個關鍵部分。<lb/>雖然歷史法醫案件中，施害者的手動作者冒充長期以來一直被認為是一種威脅，但近期大型語言模型（LLMs）的進展帶來了新的挑戰，因為對手可能利用這些工具來冒充他人的寫作。<lb/>本研究探討了被提示的LLMs是否能生成令人信服的作者冒充，以及這些輸出是否能逃避現有的法醫AV系統。<lb/>我們使用GPT-4o作為對手模型，在三種體裁下的四種提示條件下生成了冒充文本：電子郵件、短信和社交媒體帖子。<lb/>然後，我們在似然比框架內，將這些輸出與非神經AV方法（n-gram追蹤、基於排名的冒名者方法、LambdaG）以及神經方法（AdHominem、LUAR、STAR）進行了評估。<lb/>結果顯示，LLM生成的文本未能充分複製作者的個性，以繞過已建立的AV系統。<lb/>我們還觀察到，某些方法在拒絕冒充文本時的準確性甚至高於對真實負樣本的準確性。<lb/>總體而言，這些發現表明，儘管LLMs的可獲得性，當前的AV系統在多種體裁的入門級冒充嘗試中仍然保持穩健。<lb/>此外，我們證明這種反直覺的韌性部分源於LLM生成文本中固有的更高詞彙多樣性和熵。
2603.29454.author: Baoyi Zeng et.al.
